evidence: PluginManager adds URL.canParse hosts from all config strings to allowedNetworkHosts, and sandbox fetch then treats those hosts as permitted.

method: static source trace plus focused existing tests
evidence: Line chain proves config URL strings are collected into allowedNetworkHosts and passed to sandbox. Focused pluginManager derived-grants test passed, confirming config-derived grants are active runtime behavior.
proof_gap: No bespoke malicious manifest/default-config harness was added in this read-only scan subtask.

source_or_boundary: malicious plugin manifest defaults or renderer/settings plugin config
closest_control: network permission plus PluginManager host derivation from arbitrary config strings
sink_or_broken_control: sandbox fetch allowedNetworkHosts check consumes derived host grants
impact_path: Install/enable plugin with network permission and URL-shaped default config, or place URL in plugin config; PluginManager derives the host; plugin calls usp.fetch to that host without declaring it in manifest.network.allowedHosts.
severity_facts: Bypasses declared host allowlist into desktop network context; requires network permission and plugin install/enable or renderer/settings write; dynamic server URLs are intended for some plugins, but the collector is field-agnostic and default-aware.
